skill.name: scholar-evaluation
skill.description: Apply the ScholarEval framework to evaluate academic papers, proposals, literature reviews, and scholarly writing across structured quality dimensions.
skill.summary: Evaluate scholarly work with structured rubrics, scoring guidance, and prioritized revision feedback.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical heuristic combinations, weak cryptography, command, network, and credential patterns. Review found no prompt injection, no network calls, no credential access, and no executable shell invocation; those alerts are false positives from academic wording, markdown examples, a citation URL, and scores.keys(). The only confirmed concern is low-risk local filesystem access in the score calculator, which reads score files and can write a report to a user-selected path.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Read and Write
low_findings.description: The score calculator reads score and weight files from user-provided paths and can write a report to a user-provided output path. This is expected for a local CLI utility, but a mistaken path could overwrite accessible files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads and writes are directly visible in the script and are tied to explicit CLI or interactive user choices. Confidence is below very high because this is legitimate utility behavior, not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Critical Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported dangerous combination of code execution, network, and credential access was not confirmed. The reviewed files contain markdown command examples, a citation URL, local file operations, and no credential exfiltration path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations show documentation examples, an arXiv citation, and dictionary key access rather than credential or network behavior. I found no evidence of subprocess, HTTP client use, or secret harvesting in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary academic terms and rubric text, not cryptographic primitives. No hashing, encryption, or password handling code was found at the cited documentation locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is scholarly evaluation language and quality-level lookup code. There is no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive credential flow connected to these matches.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Output Path Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes reports to a path selected by the user. This should be disclosed because it can create or overwrite files within the permissions of the local process.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operation and CLI output argument are directly visible. The risk remains low because execution is user initiated and there is no hidden destination.

content.value_statement: Academic review often lacks consistent criteria across papers, proposals, and literature reviews. This skill applies ScholarEval rubrics to produce structured scores, evidence-based feedback, and revision priorities.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates academic work across problem formulation, literature review, methodology, data, analysis, results, writing, and citations.
Provides dimension-level qualitative feedback with strengths, weaknesses, and critical issues.
Supports optional 1 to 5 scoring for each evaluation dimension.
Uses a local Python script to calculate weighted aggregate scores from dimension ratings.
Assesses publication readiness and recommends prioritized revisions.
Adapts evaluation expectations by work type, development stage, venue, and discipline.
limitations: Does not replace expert peer review or discipline-specific judgment.
Quality depends on the completeness of the submitted scholarly work.
The score calculator expects prepared numeric ratings before it can compute totals.
Does not perform live literature retrieval or citation verification.

use_cases.title: Review a Manuscript Before Submission
use_cases.description: Assess a full research paper against structured criteria and identify changes needed before journal or conference submission.

use_cases.title: Evaluate a Thesis Chapter
use_cases.description: Give consistent feedback on research questions, methods, analysis, writing, and citation quality for a developing chapter.

use_cases.title: Screen Research Proposals
use_cases.description: Compare proposals using shared dimensions for feasibility, contribution, rigor, and expected scholarly value.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Paper Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one academic paper
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this research paper using the ScholarEval dimensions. Provide strengths, weaknesses, scores from 1 to 5, and three priority revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Methodology Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review only the methodology, data collection, and analysis sections. Assess rigor, reproducibility, validity, bias handling, and alignment with the research questions.

prompt_templates.title: Publication Readiness Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this manuscript for publication readiness at the target venue. Identify blocking issues, major revisions, minor revisions, and evidence for each judgment.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Proposal Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these research proposals using ScholarEval criteria. Rank them by soundness, contribution, feasibility, and clarity, then explain the tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: A draft empirical paper with unclear research questions and a strong analysis section.
output: Overall assessment: promising but not ready for submission.
Highest-scoring dimension: analysis and interpretation, because the methods match the data.
Priority revision: rewrite the research questions and connect each result to a stated question.

output_examples.input: A literature review chapter for a dissertation proposal.
output: Coverage is broad, but synthesis is limited.
The review should group sources by theme and explain disagreements in the field.
Citation quality is acceptable, but recent work from the last three years is underrepresented.

output_examples.input: Three grant proposal summaries competing for internal seed funding.
output: Proposal B has the strongest feasibility and clearest contribution.
Proposal A has high potential impact but weak data access plans.
Proposal C needs a narrower scope before it can be fairly evaluated.

best_practices: State the work type, target venue, discipline, and development stage before evaluation.
Ask for evidence from specific sections when assigning scores or recommending revisions.
Use scores as decision support, not as a replacement for expert judgment.
anti_patterns: Do not score work without reading the relevant sections in full.
Do not apply journal-level standards to early student drafts unless requested.
Do not treat generated feedback as verified peer review or citation validation.

faq.question: What types of work can this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It supports research papers, proposals, literature reviews, thesis chapters, conference abstracts, and similar scholarly writing.

faq.question: Does it verify whether citations are real?
faq.answer: No. It can assess citation quality indicators, but it does not perform live citation verification.

faq.question: Can it produce numeric scores?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports optional 1 to 5 dimension scores and includes a script for weighted aggregate scoring.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for peer review?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured evaluation support and should be combined with expert review.

faq.question: Can the rubric be adjusted by discipline?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow asks users to adjust expectations by field, venue, work type, and development stage.

faq.question: Does the skill access the network?
faq.answer: No confirmed network behavior was found. The listed URL is a citation in the documentation.
